publicationStmt.publisher: Schenker Documents Online in association with the Centre for Computing in the Humanities

sourceDesc.p: No source: created in electronic format.

encodingDesc.p: Encoding according to the CCH TEI Guidelines

div.p: Adopted daughter of Reinhard Oppel and his first wife Käthe Oppel (née Brebeck); first child of Reinhard Oppel.

Presumably when Reinhard and Käthe divorced in 1910 Ingeborg and her younger brother, Wolf Dietrich Oppel, were assigned to the custody of their mother. Neither of them features in the correspondence between Reinhard and Heinrich Schenker except that in May 1931 Reinhard reports that Ingeborg has passed her first state examination and become a candidate for higher civil service (OJ 13/10, [129]); and in that between Reinhard and Jeanette Schenker after Heinrich’s death in 1935, she is mentioned once: “Ingeborg has been obliged to serve as an assistant in the Ministry of Justice, for the development of the new juvenile law” (OJ 13/10, [192], March 3, 1935). Nothing more is known of her.
